'''Kirk Dixon''' (born 19 July 1984), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''"Dicko"''', is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|wg}} for [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] and the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

==Career==
Dixon was born in [[Kingston upon Hull|Hull]] and played rugby union for [[Hull Ionians]] before starting his rugby league career with [[Hull FC]]<ref name="YP1Sep03">{{cite web |title=Patience pays off for Ionians as Dixon inspires Cup triumph |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/patience-pays-off-for-ionians-as-dixon-inspires-cup-triumph-1-2526786 |website=The Yorkshire Post |accessdate=7 July 2019 |date=1 September 2003 |archive-date=7 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190707030353/https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/patience-pays-off-for-ionians-as-dixon-inspires-cup-triumph-1-2526786 |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="BBC8Jan15" /><ref name="BBC7Jan15" /> He briefly played rugby union for [[Northampton Saints]] before joining [[Castleford Tigers]] in October 2006 on a one-year contract.<ref name="BBC25Sep06">{{cite web| url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_union/my_club/northampton/5378854.stm|title=Saints sign league centre Dixon|publisher=BBC Sport|date=25 September 2006|access-date=7 July 2019}}</ref><ref name="BBC12Oct2006">{{cite web |title=Saints' Dixon to return to league |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/castleford/6043960.stm |publisher=BBC Sport |access-date=7 July 2019 |date=12 October 2006}}</ref> Castleford manager Mick Robinson said: "Kirk is another player with Super league experience who has a lot to offer us. He has good vision and is a real try poacher."<ref name="LRL12Oct06">{{cite web |title=Tigers Sign Dixon |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/tigers-sign-dixon/ |website=Love Rugby League |accessdate=7 July 2019 |date=12 October 2006}}</ref> Dixon scored 23 tries in the 2007 season, helping Castleford to promotion, and signed a two-year contract in October 2007.<ref name="SS11Oct07">{{cite web |title=Tigers keep hold of trio |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/2794262/tigers-keep-hold-of-trio |publisher=Sky Sports |accessdate=7 July 2019 |date=11 October 2007}}</ref>

Dixon was given a new two-year deal following some good form in 2009.<ref name="LRL7Jul09">{{cite web |title=Tiger Dixon's deal delight |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/tiger-dixons-deal-delight/ |website=Love Rugby League |accessdate=7 July 2019 |date=7 July 2009}}</ref> He finished the club's top try scorer in this season. He won the coaches player of the year for 2009. He suffered a blood clot in 2009 that ruled him out for six months.<ref name="Ind29Jan13">{{cite web |title=Injury jinx hits Kirk Dixon again |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/injury-jinx-hits-kirk-dixon-again-8471875.html |website=The Independent |accessdate=7 July 2019 |date=29 January 2013}}</ref>

Dixon signed a three-year contract in July 2011, until the end of the 2014 season.<ref name="SS4Jul11">{{cite web |title=Dixon agrees Castleford stay |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/7018509/dixon-agrees-castleford-stay |publisher=Sky Sports |accessdate=7 July 2019 |date=4 July 2011}}</ref> A shoulder injury suffered in the Challenge Cup semi-final in August 2011 ended his season.<ref name="Ind29Jan13" /><ref name=LRL10Jan12>{{cite web |title=Dixon set for return |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/dixon-set-for-return/ |website=Love Rugby League |accessdate=7 July 2019 |date=10 January 2012}}</ref> A neck injury ruled Dixon out for seven months in 2012.<ref name="Ind29Jan13" />

Dixon missed the start of the 2013 season with a calf injury but scored 11 tries in 21 games and signed a one-year extension to his contract in February 2014.<ref name="SS29Jan13">{{cite web |title=Castleford Tigers suffer injury blow as winger Kirk Dixon tears calf muscle |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/8452158/castleford-tigers-suffer-injury-blow-as-winger-kirk-dixon-tears-calf-muscle |publisher=Sky Sports |accessdate=7 July 2019 |date=29 January 2013}}</ref><ref name="LRL20Aug13">{{cite web |title=1000 points for Dixon |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/1000-points-for-dixon/ |website=Love Rugby League |accessdate=7 July 2019 |date=20 August 2013}}</ref><ref name="SS13Feb14">{{cite web |title=Super League: Kirk Dixon extends contract with Castleford Tigers |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/9163857/super-league-kirk-dixon-extends-contract-with-castleford-tigers |publisher=Sky Sports |accessdate=7 July 2019 |date=13 February 2014}}</ref> [[Daryl Powell]] described him as "...the ultimate professional [who] works harder than anyone".<ref name="SS16Feb14">{{cite web |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers' Kirk Dixon hailed as 'the ultimate pro' |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/9170023/super-league-castleford-tigers-kirk-dixon-hailed-as-the-ultimate-pro |publisher=Sky Sports |accessdate=7 July 2019 |date=16 February 2014}}</ref>

He played in the [[2014 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall’s double stuns Castleford">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall's double stuns Castleford|work=The Guardian|date=23 August 2014|accessdate=13 November 2019}}</ref>

Prior to the start of the 2015 season, Dixon announced his retirement following surgery to his neck.<ref name=BBC7Jan15>{{cite web|title=Kirk Dixon: Castleford Tigers man forced to retire with neck injury|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/30710084|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=18 January 2015|date=7 January 2015}}</ref><ref name="YP6Jan15">{{cite web |last1=Craven |first1=Dave |title=Health now the priority as Dixon calls time on his career |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers/health-now-the-priority-as-dixon-calls-time-on-his-career-1-7036773 |website=The Yorkshire Post |accessdate=7 July 2019 |date=6 January 2015}}</ref> He had scored 70 tries and 269 goals in 158 Super League games, and scored more than 1,000 points for Castleford.<ref name="BBC8Jan15">{{cite web |title=Kirk Dixon: Castleford respect swayed decision to retire |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/30725499 |publisher=BBC Sport |accessdate=7 July 2019 |date=8 January 2015}}</ref>
